questions.text: The Channel Tunnel officially opened, connecting Britain to mainland Europe for the first time.
England won the FIFA World Cup, beating West Germany 4–2 in the final at Wembley.
The Beatles released their debut album Please Please Me, recorded in a single day.
Margaret Thatcher became Britain's first female Prime Minister after a Conservative election victory.
Queen Elizabeth II celebrated her Diamond Jubilee, marking 60 years on the throne.
Scientists at the Roslin Institute in Edinburgh announced the cloning of Dolly the Sheep.
Concorde made its first commercial flights, operated jointly by British Airways and Air France.
Andy Murray became the first British man to win Wimbledon in 77 years.
Oasis released their landmark album Definitely Maybe, the fastest-selling debut in UK chart history at the time.
Tony Blair led Labour to a landslide victory, ending 18 years of Conservative government.
Prince Charles married Lady Diana Spencer at St Paul's Cathedral in London.
Only Fools and Horses first aired on BBC One, introducing Del Boy and Rodney to the nation.
The Great Fire of London broke out in a bakery on Pudding Lane, burning for four days.
Torvill and Dean won Olympic gold at Sarajevo with their iconic Bolero routine.
Queen performed their legendary set at Live Aid at Wembley Stadium.
Brexit — the United Kingdom officially left the European Union.
Queen Elizabeth II passed away at Balmoral Castle, Scotland, ending a 70-year reign.
Tim Berners-Lee, a British scientist, invented the World Wide Web at CERN.
